title: Tarka Dal
description: My Thermomix take on Madhur Jaffrey's tarka dal — creamy lentils with a sizzling spiced topping.
intro: Madhur Jaffrey taught the world to cook Indian food at home. Her tarka dal is pure comfort — soft lentils finished with a fragrant tempered-spice topping. The Thermomix cooks the lentils hands-free.
ingredients: For the tarka: 30 g ghee or oil
steps: Place the lentils, water, turmeric and salt in the bowl. Cook 20 min / 100°C / speed 1, basket on the lid. Blend 5 sec / 0°C / speed 4 for a creamy dal, then pour into a serving bowl.
For the tarka, place the onion and garlic in the bowl and chop 4 sec / 0°C / speed 5.
Add the ghee, cumin seeds, dried chilli and tomato and sauté 6 min / 120°C / speed 1.
Stir the tarka through the dal and serve with rice or flatbread; finish 1 min / 0°C / no speed.
tips: The tarka — tempered spices poured over at the end — is the soul of the dish.
A squeeze of lemon and fresh coriander lift it beautifully.

faq.a: Red lentils (masoor dal) cook fastest and go lovely and creamy.
Stir a spoon of butter or coconut milk through at the end.

faq.q: Is this the original Madhur Jaffrey recipe?
faq.a: No. This is Chef Aly’s independent Thermomix reinterpretation inspired by Madhur Jaffrey and tarka dal; it is not endorsed by or affiliated with the chef.
